heading: State partnership planning and demonstration grants to support meaningful relationships between foster children and the incarcerated parents of the children

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary may make demonstration grants to eligible State partnerships to develop, implement, and provide support for programs that enable and sustain meaningful relationships between covered foster children and the incarcerated parents of the children.

children.heading: Payment of annual installments
children.label: 2 Payment of annual installments
children.text: The Secretary shall pay each demonstration grant in 5 annual installments.

children.label: A
children.text: the recipient includes a request for a planning grant in the application under subsection (c); and

children.label: B
children.text: the Secretary determines that a planning grant would assist the recipient and improve the effectiveness of the demonstration grant.

children.label: A
children.text: The State child welfare agency responsible for the administration of the State plans under this part.

children.label: B
children.text: The State agency responsible for adult corrections.

children.heading: Additional partners
children.label: 2 Additional partners
children.text: For purposes of this section, an eligible State partnership may include any entity with experience in serving incarcerated parents and their children.

children.heading: Partnerships entered into by Indian tribes or tribal consortia
children.label: 3 Partnerships entered into by Indian tribes or tribal consortia
children.text: Notwithstanding paragraph (1), if an Indian tribe or tribal consortium enters into a partnership pursuant to this section that does not consist solely of tribal child welfare agencies (or a consortium of the agencies), the partnership shall be considered an eligible State partnership for purposes of this section.
A summary of the program, including how the program will support a meaningful relationship between a covered foster child and an incarcerated parent of the child.
A description of the activities to be carried out by the program, which must include all of the activities described in subsection (d) that are in the best interest of the covered foster child.

children.label: A
children.text: each covered foster child eligible for services under the program, including, to the extent practicable, coordination of data between relevant State child welfare agencies and court systems; and

children.label: B
children.text: the roles and responsibilities of the entities in the partnership.
Documentation that the applicant is an eligible State partnership.

Assurances that the applicant will participate fully in the evaluation described in subsection (f)(2) and shall maintain records for the program, including demographic information disaggregated by relevant characteristics with respect to covered foster children and incarcerated parents who participate in the program.

children.label: i
children.text: inclusion of parents in case planning and decision making for children;

children.label: ii
children.text: regular sharing of information and responses to requests for information between caseworkers and incarcerated parents with respect to the case information of a child, any changes to a case, permanency plans, requirements to maintain parental rights, and any efforts to terminate parental rights;

children.label: iii
children.text: appropriate opportunities for incarcerated parents to demonstrate their relationship with a covered foster child given their incarceration, including training and courses required for a service plan; and

children.label: iv
children.text: the enhanced visitation described in paragraph (2).

children.label: I
children.text: the ability to physically touch parents;

children.label: II
children.text: engaging with parents in locations that are appropriate for the age and development of the child;

children.label: III
children.text: exchanging items that are appropriate to the age and development of the child, include expectations that are appropriate for the age and development of the child related to behavior, attire, and wait times; and

children.label: IV
children.text: allowing appropriate adults to bring children if legal guardians are not available to promote regular contact;

children.label: ii
children.text: reasonable inclusion of all children of the parent;

children.label: iii
children.text: communication and visitation at times when the children are available;

children.label: iv
children.text: security procedures to comfort children and be minimally invasive; and

children.label: v
children.text: promoting parent-child relationships regardless of the sentence imposed on the parent.

children.label: A
children.text: Grantees shall facilitate weekly communication and, for at least 9 days each year, in-person visitation between a covered foster child and any incarcerated parent of the child.

children.label: B
children.text: Electronic visitation (such as live video visits, phone calls, and recorded books) may be used but shall not be the sole method to promote a meaningful relationship for purposes of the grant.

children.label: i
children.text: integrate best practices for visitation programs with incarcerated parents and their children;

children.label: ii
children.text: adopt developmentally appropriate visitation policies and procedures such as those described in paragraph (1)(B);

children.label: iii
children.text: reduce or eliminate the cost of developmentally appropriate communication and visitation for the covered foster child, which may include the purchase of communication technology, covering transportation, insurance, and lodging costs, costs related to providing appropriate visitation spaces and activities, and other relevant costs;

children.label: iv
children.text: to the extent practicable, integrate appropriate parenting education to help prepare and process visits; and

children.label: v
children.text: avoid restricting visitation and communication as a punishment for the incarcerated parents.

children.heading: Training
children.label: 3 Training
children.text: Grantees shall incorporate ongoing training for child welfare workers, correctional facility staff, and other program providers to understand the importance of promoting meaningful relationships between children and incarcerated parents.

children.heading: Case management
children.label: 4 Case management
children.text: Grantees shall provide case management services for the incarcerated parents of a covered foster child to promote the relationship, access to services, and coordination with the caseworkers of the covered foster child to strengthen the relationship.

children.heading: Legal assistance
children.label: 5 Legal assistance
children.text: Grantees shall facilitate access to necessary legal services and may use grant funds for services that are not reimbursable under other Federal programs.

children.heading: Federal share
children.label: e Federal share
children.text: The Federal share of the cost of any activity carried out using a grant made under this section shall be not greater than 75 percent.

children.heading: Technical assistance, evaluations, and reports
children.label: f Technical assistance, evaluations, and reports
A
children.text: assisting grantees in understanding best practices in promoting meaningful relationships between incarcerated parents and their children as well as consulting with appropriate stakeholders when developing their programs;

children.label: B
children.text: assisting grantees with establishing and analyzing implementation and performance indicators; and

children.label: C
children.text: conducting an annual technical assistance and training meeting and an annual grantee meeting so that grantees can learn from the experiences of other grantees.

children.heading: Evaluations
children.label: 2 Evaluations
children.text: The Secretary shall conduct an evaluation of program outcomes, including with respect to parent and child well-being, parent-child interactions, parental involvement, awareness of child development and parenting practices, placement stability, and termination of parental rights with respect to covered foster children and incarcerated parents, to measure program effectiveness, as determined by the Secretary, and identify opportunities for improved program practices and implementation.

children.label: i
children.text: the number of applications for grants under this section;

children.label: ii
children.text: the number of grants awarded, and the amounts for each grant; and

children.label: I
children.text: interim results of the evaluation described in paragraph (2);

children.label: II
children.text: disaggregated data on covered foster children and incarcerated parents;

children.label: III
children.text: information on the composition of eligible State partnerships;

children.label: IV
children.text: best practices for facilitating meaningful relationships between covered foster children and incarcerated parents; and

children.label: V
children.text: barriers to implementation or expansion of programs funded under this section.

children.label: i
children.text: the final results of the evaluation described in paragraph (2); and

children.label: ii
children.text: recommendations for refinements to grant requirements to improve program outcomes.

children.heading: Authority of Secretary with respect to Indian tribes and tribal organizations
children.label: g Authority of Secretary with respect to Indian tribes and tribal organizations

children.heading: Waiver or modification of requirements
children.label: 1 Waiver or modification of requirements
children.text: In making a grant to an Indian tribe or tribal organization under this section, the Secretary may waive the matching requirement of subsection (e) or modify an application requirement imposed by or under subsection (c) if the Secretary determines that the waiver or modification is appropriate to the needs, culture, and circumstances of the Indian tribe or tribal organization.

children.heading: Evaluation
children.label: 2 Evaluation
children.text: The Secretary shall use tribally relevant data in carrying out the evaluation under subsection (f)(2) with respect to an Indian tribe or tribal organization.

children.heading: Limitations on authorization of appropriations
children.label: h Limitations on authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to the Secretary not more than $35,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2026 through 2029 to carry out this section.

children.heading: Definition of covered foster child
children.label: i Definition of covered foster child

children.text: is in foster care; and
has at least 1 parent incarcerated in a Federal, State, or local correctional facility.
